query_normalized: can publicly available data reveal subtle violations of time-reversal symmetry in beta decay? physics
rationale: The paper focuses on ab initio calculations of standard allowed beta decays (Fermi and Gamow-Teller) using the In-Medium Similarity Renormalization Group, which is a method for modeling nuclear structure and decay rates, but it does not address the specific mechanism of "time-reversal symmetry violations" nor the use of "publicly available data" to detect subtle symmetry breaking.
